Huly Issue: MXSYN-18

title: Implement retry logic for failed conversions

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Objective

Add robust error handling and retry logic for document processing.

## Implementation

1. Retry failed Docling requests (max 3 attempts)
2. Exponential backoff between retries
3. Queue failed jobs for later retry
4. Alert on persistent failures

## Error Scenarios

- Docling service unavailable
- Timeout on large documents
- Corrupt/invalid file format
- Rate limiting

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Transient failures auto-retry
- [ ] Persistent failures logged and alerted
- [ ] User notified of final failure status
- [ ] Failed jobs can be manually retried

---

# Streaming Tests

###

###

- [ ] Long-running operation with background mode
- [ ] Connection recovery simulation

### Test Scenarios

1. Simple prompt → Single assistant response
2. Search prompt → tool_call → tool_return → assistant
3. Complex prompt → Multiple tool calls → Multiple returns → assistant
4. Error scenario → LLM error → Graceful failure
5. Long operation → Background mode → Recovery

## Manual Testing Checklist

- [ ] Basic message in Matrix triggers streaming
- [ ] Tool progress visible during execution
- [ ] Final response correct
- [ ] No duplicate messages
- [ ] Error messages clear
- [ ] Works with different agents
- [ ] Fallback works when streaming disabled

- Agent with conversation_search tool
- Agent with multiple tools
- Agent with long-running tools

Huly Issue: MXSYN-1

title: Add environment configuration for file upload integration
Create Matrix notification helpers for file reservations
Test end-to-end multi-agent file coordination

comments.author: node
comments.text:
Comprehensive testing of the three-layer architecture with real agent workflows.

## Test Scenarios

1. **Scenario 1: Successful Reservation**
   - OpenCode agent reserves 
   - Letta agent checks for conflicts → sees reservation
   - Letta agent picks different file
   - Both complete work without conflicts
2. **Scenario 2: Pre-commit Guard**
   - Agent A reserves file
   - Agent B tries to commit changes to same file
   - Pre-commit guard blocks commit
   - Agent B sees clear error message
3. **Scenario 3: Matrix Notifications**
   - Agent reserves files
   - Reservation appears in Matrix room
   - Human sees notification in Element
   - Agent releases → release notification appears
4. **Scenario 4: Huly Integration**
   - Agent reserves files with reason=MXSYN-XXX
   - Reservation linked to Huly issue
   - Audit trail shows issue context

## Success Criteria

- All scenarios pass
- Zero file conflicts
- Clear visibility in Matrix
- Pre-commit guards work correctly

## Parent

Blocked by: Document MCP Agent Mail integration

---
Huly Issue: MXSYN-56

title: Add monitoring and alerting for file processing

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Objective

Implement observability for the file processing pipeline.

## Metrics to Track

- Files processed (daily/weekly)
- Processing time distribution
- Error rate by file type
- Queue depth (pending files)
- Docling worker health

## Monitoring Stack

- Flower UI for Celery monitoring (http://192.168.50.90:3058)
- Custom metrics endpoint
- Alert on high error rate or queue backup

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Dashboard showing key metrics
- [ ] Alerts for failures > 5%
- [ ] Alerts for queue > 10 items
- [ ] Historical data retention

Huly Issue: MXSYN-16

title: Performance optimization for document processing

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Objective

Optimize processing pipeline for speed and resource efficiency.

## Optimizations

1. Parallel processing for multiple uploads
2. Caching for repeated document uploads
3. Optimize Docling worker configuration
4. Stream large files instead of loading to memory

## Targets

- Processing time < 30s for 10-page PDF
- Support 5+ concurrent conversions
- Memory usage < 500MB per conversion

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Benchmark current performance
- [ ] Implement optimizations
- [ ] Verify targets met
- [ ] Document configuration tuning

---

Huly Issue: MXSYN-18

title: Phase 4: Investigate Token Streaming (Deferred)

comments.author: node
comments.text:
# Token Streaming Investigation

## Status: DEFERRED

Token streaming () is currently not returning content chunks from the Letta server.

## Current Behavior

When :

- Only  and  events received
- No  or  chunks
- Same behavior on direct API (port 8283) and proxy (port 8289)

## Expected Behavior (per Letta docs)

Should receive multiple chunks with same message ID:



## Investigation Tasks

- [ ] Test with different agents/models (GPT-4o, Gemini)
- [ ] Check Letta server version
- [ ] Review server logs for errors
- [ ] Test on Letta Cloud vs self-hosted
- [ ] Check if Claude Sonnet 4 supports token streaming via Letta

## Potential Causes

1. **Model limitation**: Claude Sonnet 4 may not support token streaming through Letta
2. **Server configuration**: Token streaming may need to be enabled
3. **Agent configuration**: May need specific agent settings
4. **SDK version**: May need SDK update

## Impact if Not Fixed

- Step streaming provides adequate UX for most use cases
- Token streaming mainly improves perceived latency
- Not blocking for MVP

## References

- Letta docs: https://docs.letta.com/guides/agents/streaming/
- Test script:

title: Phase 2: Integrate Streaming into Matrix Client

Agent Mail Reverse Bridge: Handle edge cases
description:
## Overview
Handle edge cases and failure scenarios in the reverse bridge.

## Edge Cases
1. **Agent sends multiple messages** - Forward all or just first?
2. **Agent uses tool calls** - Forward tool results or just final response?
3. **Agent mentions other users** - Preserve mentions?
4. **Message contains attachments/images** - Forward or skip?
5. **Rate limiting** - Don't spam Agent Mail
6. **Duplicate detection** - Don't forward same response twice

## Failure Handling
- Agent Mail API unavailable → queue and retry
- Invalid code name mapping → log error, skip
- Thread_id missing → create new thread or skip?

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Multi-message responses handled appropriately
- [ ] Duplicates prevented
- [ ] Failures logged and recoverable
- [ ] Rate limiting in place

Huly Issue: MXSYN-16

title: Agent Mail Bidirectional Bridge: Architecture decision
description:
## Overview
Decide where the reverse bridge logic should live.

## Options

### Option A: Extend existing agent_mail_bridge.py
- Add Matrix listener alongside Agent Mail poller
- Single container, shared state
- Simpler deployment
- Risk: complexity in one service

### Option B: Separate reverse bridge service
- New container: agent_mail_reverse_bridge
- Subscribes to Matrix events
- Calls Agent Mail API independently
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- More containers to manage

### Option C: Add to matrix-client
- matrix-client already processes agent responses
- Add hook after agent response sent to Matrix
- Check if original was from bridge, forward if so
- Least new code, leverages existing flow

## Recommendation
Option C seems cleanest - matrix-client already has the context.

## Decision Needed
- [ ] Choose architecture approach
- [ ] Document rationale

Huly Issue: MXSYN-2

title: Agent Mail Reverse Bridge: Track bridge-originated messages
description:
## Overview
Track which Matrix messages originated from the Agent Mail bridge so we can identify when agents respond to them.

## Requirements
- When bridge sends a message to Matrix, store metadata mapping:
  - Matrix event_id → Agent Mail message_id
  - Matrix room_id → Agent Mail recipient (code name)
  - Original sender (code name)
- Storage options: in-memory dict, SQLite, or Redis
- Must survive bridge restarts (persistent storage preferred)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bridge stores mapping when forwarding messages
- [ ] Mapping includes: matrix_event_id, agent_mail_msg_id, sender, recipient, thread_id
- [ ] Mapping persists across restarts

title: Implement Letta file upload with metadata
Phase 1: Implement Step Streaming Adapter
Agent Mail Reverse Bridge: Forward replies back to Agent Mail
description:
## Overview
When an agent responds to a bridge message, forward that response back to Agent Mail as a reply.

## Requirements
- Use Agent Mail `reply_message` API to maintain thread
- Map Matrix user back to Agent Mail code name
- Include original context in the reply

## Message Mapping
- Matrix agent user (@agent_xxx) → Agent Mail code name (WhiteStone, etc)
- Use existing agent_mail_mappings.json or agent_user_mappings.json

## Reply Format
- Preserve the agent's response text
- Include metadata: responded via Matrix, timestamp
- Maintain thread_id from original message

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Agent responses forwarded to Agent Mail via reply_message
- [ ] Thread continuity maintained
- [ ] Sender correctly mapped to Agent Mail identity
- [ ] Original sender receives notification

title: Phase 4: Investigate Token Streaming (Deferred)

comments.author: node
comments.text:
# Token Streaming Investigation

## Status: DEFERRED

Token streaming () is currently not returning content chunks from the Letta server.

## Current Behavior

When :

- Only  and  events received
- No  or  chunks
- Same behavior on direct API (port 8283) and proxy (port 8289)

## Expected Behavior (per Letta docs)

Should receive multiple chunks with same message ID:



## Investigation Tasks

- [ ] Test with different agents/models (GPT-4o, Gemini)
- [ ] Check Letta server version
- [ ] Review server logs for errors
- [ ] Test on Letta Cloud vs self-hosted
- [ ] Check if Claude Sonnet 4 supports token streaming via Letta

## Potential Causes

1. **Model limitation**: Claude Sonnet 4 may not support token streaming through Letta
2. **Server configuration**: Token streaming may need to be enabled
3. **Agent configuration**: May need specific agent settings
4. **SDK version**: May need SDK update

## Impact if Not Fixed

- Step streaming provides adequate UX for most use cases
- Token streaming mainly improves perceived latency
- Not blocking for MVP

## References

- Letta docs: https://docs.letta.com/guides/agents/streaming/

Huly Issue: MXSYN-1

title: Implement retry logic for failed conversions

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Objective

Add robust error handling and retry logic for document processing.

## Implementation

1. Retry failed Docling requests (max 3 attempts)
2. Exponential backoff between retries
3. Queue failed jobs for later retry
4. Alert on persistent failures

## Error Scenarios

- Docling service unavailable
- Timeout on large documents
- Corrupt/invalid file format
- Rate limiting

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Transient failures auto-retry
- [ ] Persistent failures logged and alerted
- [ ] User notified of final failure status
- [ ] Failed jobs can be manually retried

---

title: Add OCR support for scanned PDFs and images

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Objective

Enable OCR extraction for scanned documents and images.

## Implementation

1. Detect if PDF is scanned (no selectable text)
2. Enable OCR in Docling request for images (PNG, JPG)
3. Handle mixed documents (some pages scanned, some not)

## Supported Formats

- Scanned PDFs
- PNG images
- JPG/JPEG images

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] OCR extracts text from scanned PDFs
- [ ] Images converted to searchable Markdown
- [ ] OCR accuracy > 95% for clear scans
- [ ] Processing time < 30s for typical 10-page document

Huly Issue: MXSYN-50

title: Agent Mail Reverse Bridge: Detect agent replies to bridge messages
description:
## Overview
Detect when a Letta agent responds to a message that came from the Agent Mail bridge.

## Requirements
- Monitor Matrix rooms for messages from Letta agents
- Check if the message is a reply (m.relates_to.m.in_reply_to)
- Look up the replied-to event_id in the bridge tracking store
- If found, this is a response that needs to be forwarded back

## Detection Logic
1. Agent posts message in room
2. Check if message has reply_to event_id
3. Query tracking store for that event_id
4. If match found → trigger reverse forward

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detect agent replies to bridge messages
- [ ] Handle both direct replies and thread replies
- [ ] Ignore agent messages that aren't replies to bridge messages

All batches landed: streaming/fallback, core recovery, document_parser, identity-bridge, tests, ops/infra. 10 commits pushed to main.

Gateway URL default hardened to explicit configuration with fail-fast behavior
Standardized retry/backoff to shared utility across auth, streaming, document parsing, and bridge wrappers with test coverage; full unit suite green.

Reworked greptile-review.sh argv parsing to support --watch as a boolean flag and positional PR number in any order; added strict unknown-option/multi-PR validation and verified script parsing/usage paths.

Updated /messages/recent aggregation to pass caller-provided limit into per-room get_messages calls (with safe fallback of 50 for non-positive limits), and added endpoint tests validating both forwarded and fallback limits.
Implemented claim-to-citation mapping in document overview API with structured citation payload and tests
Added Matrix thread context propagation for streaming tool-call workflows with fallback reply metadata and full unit regression coverage
Implemented explicit room power level defaults, dynamic get/set power level APIs, read-only/writable helpers, and multi-agent role hierarchy with passing unit coverage
Resolved by baking reply_to_event_id into the send_message closure (9s3 fix)
All 8 child beads completed. 7 monoliths decomposed, critical test gaps filled.

Shipped: Path 1 room-registered delivery now runs through isAdmissibleForRoomDelivery (opencode-bridge/src/sender-filter.ts). Dropped dead wasMessageSentByUs/trackOutboundMessage machinery. Live-verified on restart: agent bot @agent_c8c4ed19... dropped in !MtuqLUCwvKypRU6gll (the room that was looping).

Shipped: initMatrix is now supervised with exponential backoff (1s→30s, retries forever). Fire-and-forget from main() so HTTP/WS start accepting regardless of Matrix state. On live restart: resolved identity + client ready in one attempt.

Shipped: bridge-side per-room token bucket (20 burst, 5/sec sustained) in handleOutboundMessage via opencode-bridge/src/rate-limiter.ts. Aggregated drop log every 5s. Plugin-side coalescing intentionally skipped — bridge cap is the real safety net.
